content.problemStatement:
Chef is given two strings and wants to make them **exactly identical** using delete operations.

In one operation, Chef can delete **exactly one character** from **either** of the two strings.

Chef wants to determine the **minimum number of delete operations** required to make both strings the same.

Function DeclarationFunction NameminimumDeleteSteps\texttt{minimumDeleteSteps}minimumDeleteSteps

Parameters
a\texttt{a}a (string):
b\texttt{b}b (string):
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the **minimum number of delete operations** required to make both strings identical.
The returned value is a **non-negative integer**.
Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \le T \le 10^51≤T≤105
1≤∣a∣,∣b∣≤5001 \le |\texttt{a}|, |\texttt{b}| \le 5001≤∣a∣,∣b∣≤500
Both a\texttt{a}a and b\texttt{b}b consist only of lowercase English letters.
Input Format
The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of **two lines**:

The first line contains the string a\texttt{a}a.
The second line contains the string b\texttt{b}b.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer: 

the minimum number of delete operations Chef needs to perform to make both strings identical.
constraints: All characters are lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains string a, the second line contains string b. Both strings contain only lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer on its own line – the minimum number of delete operations required to make the two strings identical.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
abcde
ace
hello
world
aabbcc
abcabc
sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: LCS of "abcde" and "ace" is "ace" (length 3). Deletions = (5-3)+(3-3)=2.\nTest case 2: LCS of "hello" and "world" is "lo" (length 2). Deletions = (5-2)+(5-2)=6.\nTest case 3: LCS of "aabbcc" and "abcabc" can be "abcc" (length 4). Deletions = (6-4)+(6-4)=4.
Both strings are identical, LCS length 1, no deletions needed.
No common characters, LCS length 0. All characters must be deleted: 3+3=6.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.input:
3
abcde
ace
hello
world
aabbcc
abcabc

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by finding the length of the longest common subsequence (LCS) of the two strings and then subtracting this length from the sum of the lengths of the two strings. This is because the minimum number of delete operations required to make the two strings identical is equal to the total number of characters in the two strings minus the length of their LCS.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * min(|a|, |b|) * max(|a|, |b|)) per test case, O(T * N^2) total where N is the maximum length of a string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(min(|a|, |b|)) — the space required to store the lengths of the LCS.
